example_reviews: Great for people who would like to get an idea of what is going on with them and a general idea of therapy as well. This app is helping me understand myself and helping me prepare for therapy.
Helping me understand my emotions.
It's helping me understand myself better
I really like the app! It let's you take some time off your regular doings and think for moment about yourself and how you feel
I love this app. It has helped me a lot, in terms of helping me admit and validate what I was feeling. The questions helped me be in check with myself and my feelings.
It helps me realise how im actually feeling
This app has helped me understand what I'm feeling atm and what im feeling throughout the day
It helps me understand myself better and helps me understand what im going through. Overall I love this app.
groups.notes: I removed example reviews that were about usability issues like restrictive options or missed notifications because those are better handled in the challenges group. I selected  representative examples that showcase how users feel the app helps them recognise, validate and understand their feelings.

example_reviews: I absolutely love this app
Absolutely love this app it's very helpful
I love this app. It helps me with my moods, and figure out what I'm actually feeling. I love the questions it asks, how it makes you think about what you've been feeling.
Great app, very helpful!
Great app. Really insightful!
Very useful for mental well‑being.
It's a really good app
Really helpful, would suggest it to anyone!
groups.notes: I simplified the theme name to 'General satisfaction and praise' so it's clear this group is about positive feedback. I removed examples mentioning cost concerns or lack of personal motivation (like willpower) since those fit better in the value/usage groups. I added more examples to show the breadth of praise—from simple 'love this app' comments to notes about its helpfulness and usefulness.

example_reviews: This app has helped me so much in my life. It enables me to keep track of my mental health without me having to put what I feel into words. The questions that you're asked not only help you track your state of mind and your place in life, but they also open your eyes to the way your mind and body are responding to your mental illness. It helps you realise when and why you need help.
This app helps me track how I'm doing throughout each day. As someone who struggles with depression, it's helpful for me to notice any patterns and reflect on what is different when I'm doing well.

I started using this app to help track my moods and symptoms for my therapists after severe trauma. It tracks everything and writes a summary of your mental health for you let doctors, as well as giving you insights to improve.
Very helpful for anxiety and depression!

It helps me track my depression and anxiety as well as bipolar. I'd love to but the app but it says 25.oo$ maybe you should lower the cost of fully purchasing the app, because it is truly one of the most helpful apps I have found.
Helps me keep track of my mood/emotions and possible causes of distress.
It helps to keep track of my emotional status and moodset.
I like this app, it helps keep track of my emotions and the different triggers of all the emotions. Helps with reflection and does give good insights.
groups.notes: I sharpened the focus on mood tracking and mental‑health insights, discarding generic praise or pricing debates. The examples emphasise how the app helps track emotions, identify patterns, provide summaries for therapy and support management of conditions like depression, anxiety and bipolar disorder. the examples provide a clearer picture of why users rely on the app for monitoring their mental health.

groups.theme: App effectiveness, usability and value for money
example_reviews: Terrific app--free version *very* useful! If you are feeling poor or stingy, use the free version until you know it's worth $10/month for you. If you are not limited by finances, go ahead & buy into the paid version @ $50/6 months.
The app is helpful. I just wish it had more precise options even in the free version.

Gives great insight and is easy to use. I am bad at keeping up with things, so I'm only using the free version, but if i can keep up with it, i will buy the full version... however, the free version offers enough to build up insights
Great free mood tracker with actually helpful questions. I haven't tried the paid version bc the free version does what I needed.
cool app but wish the free version had more features
Even the free version is really insightful
Almost all courses are unavailable in free version, but the other stuff is cool
Very easy to use and helpful to track mood. Also easy to use to track other things. The questions are simple and genuinely take a minute or so to answer.
Insightful, easy to use and helpful
Easy to use and helpful with the insights it gives
The app was amazing, it really tracks my mental health and so far for me its accurate. It really help me a lot, the free already help me a lot. But i can tell you the paid version its worth it.

This app really helped me reflect on myself and track my mood day by day. The reminders three times a day are helpful and the insights help understand my mind. I really love it though I couldn't afford the premium version.
groups.notes: I expanded the theme to include usability and value for money to capture feedback on how easy the app is to use and whether the free vs paid versions meet expectations. I removed overly generic praise, leaving that for the satisfaction group, and brought in comments about insightfulness, ease of use, and comparisons between the free and full versions.

groups.theme: Challenges: paywall, technical issues and early user feedback
example_reviews: This seems like a great app, but I don't understand why not just with this app but many like it, make you pay to get more help. If someone is really hurting and they are looking for some private help on a stress free app, they don't need to be paying for it. I struggle with a lot it seems with the parts that are locked. I can't just pay however much on an app to get the help I need. I just wish everything was free so others can get the help they deserve.

I wish that these kinds of apps didn't have 'Premium' or 'Plus' because, at least for me, I can't afford or get easy access to any sort of therapist and there have been times when I've needed one. Mental health apps are supposed to help you and if you can't have access to all the services, they're not going to help you as much as they could/should. I really hate that it has a paywall of sorts.

I would have given this app 5 stars. But, when I moved to a new phone all of the 2 months of progress I had on this app was completely gone. And I was immediately discouraged to continue using this app. The app is great, though the questions tend to be repetitive.

The app isn't offering questions to answer, it's just a blank screen. I couldn't submit any entries, even though I got notified. I uninstalled and installed it again, and the app says I have no internet connection.
Good app, but I haven't got the promised result after 14 days and the questions 3x a day are getting annoying without any result.

Loved this app, used it for half a year and I'm not sure if it's cause I have a new phone or the update but ever since the update i havent been able to get any notifications even though I enabled them. because of that i always forget to check in and i miss so many days. Other than that it's a brilliant app absolutley adore it!

Update* this is my 2nd update and had to delete my previous post to write this one. It no longer works. It gives me a notification and when I open it to put in my answers there are no questions, just a black screen.
Notification stopped working. Tried everything and reached out to support as well. This is causing more anxiety than help.
groups.notes: I reorganised the previous 'Challenges and Limitations' group into a clearer collection of issues: subscription paywalls, technical problems and feedback from early users. Positive praise that was in  the automated grouping was dropped because it belongs in the praise group. The expanded examples show not only frustration with paywalls but also specific bugs (such as lost data after an update or blank screens) and complaints about repetitive questions or missing notifications.
